targets.cycle_reason: Signal strong on Tudor BB GMT Pepsi; April report shows two sells above $3,100 net and momentum unchanged from March. Operator has a confirmed buyer at $3,250.
Ceramic dial variant momentum building on Chrono24. Operator sourced two in Q1 and both closed in under 18 days.
Evergreen anchor reference. Hold steady on normal signal; trim if we see a premium compression.

cycle_focus.notes: Lead with Tudor GMT and Omega SMD. Rolex Submariner is defensive inventory, not a push target. Operator has $22k deployable; if we clear two deals in the first 10 days we refresh capital and re-run.

# Cycle Brief — cycle_2026-04

## Headline

Tudor GMT steel momentum continues; Omega SMD ceramic is the surprise
of the cycle. Defensive anchor on Rolex Submariner.

## Targets

| Ref | Brand | Model | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 79830RB | Tudor | BB GMT Pepsi | Strong signal, confirmed buyer at $3,250 |
| 210.30.42.20.01.001 | Omega | SMD 300M Ceramic | Q1 flips averaged 18 days |
| 126610LN | Rolex | Submariner Date | Evergreen anchor, hold steady |

## Capital

- Cycle target: **$22,000 deployable**
- Volume target: **6 units**
- Margin floor: **5% net** (standard)

## Brand Emphasis

- **Lead**: Tudor, Omega
- **Pullback**: none this cycle

## Follow-ups

- Re-run if we clear two deals in first 10 days and capital refreshes.
- Watch Chrono24 pricing drift on 210.30 variants — we may lift the override.
